So there used to be the Iron Curtain, which was a very, very long electrified fence in between the two countries during the Cold War. And at the end of the Cold War, the Czech and the German authorities, they established a nature reserve.

Going across the country, they thought this is a really nice idea. We'll have this huge habitat, all sorts of species. We'll have lots of room to play in. and there'll be more animals as a result and it'll all be good for biodiversity. It turns out 20 years later that deer on the Czech side just will not cross that border.
